name: Chocolate Chili
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Olive oil; or veg oil
2 cups Pinto beans, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook meat about 20 minutes until it loses pink color but is not browned (I personally don't know what that means, since it goes from brown to pink). Transfer to a bowl.
2. Heat oil in the same pan and sautee onions for 10 to 20 minutes, until translucent.
3. Stir meat into onions. Add tomato juice (I used puree instead because I had it), 3 cups of water, chili powder, cumin, oregano, cocoa, cinnamon, and salt. Blend. Bring to boil, uncovered, for an hour.
4. Taste for seasoning; add more chili powder if you like (in my opinion, you can add more of anything if you like).
5. Stir in the garlic, cornmeal, and beans. Simmer for 10 minutes. Serve with garnishes (I usually serve rice but did this for a change and loved it).
